Container.Name: GMEngine
Container.Overview: GM Engine — Unraid backend services for the GM Engine roleplay application. Provides embedding model inference (Ollama), image generation (ComfyUI), web search (SearXNG), and optional video generation (Wan2.1). Requires an Nvidia GPU. Models are stored on your cache SSD for fast load times. Install the Nvidia Driver plugin before using this container.

Config.Name: LoRA Download Service Port
Config.Description: Internal port for the LoRA download service. Used by GM Engine setup wizard to trigger downloads. No need to change unless port 8765 conflicts.

Config.Name: Ollama (Embeddings) Port
Config.Description: Port for the Ollama embedding model service. Must match the port configured in GM Engine on your local machine.

Config.Name: ComfyUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the ComfyUI image generation interface. You can also access ComfyUI directly in your browser to install models and test workflows.

Config.Name: SearXNG Port
Config.Description: Port for the SearXNG web search service.

Config.Name: Wan2.1 Port
Config.Description: Port for the Wan2.1 video generation service. Only used if Wan2.1 is enabled.

Config.Name: Model Storage Path
Config.Description: Path on your Unraid server where models are stored. STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: use a path on your cache SSD, not the array. Models load significantly faster from SSD. Ollama and ComfyUI models will be stored here.

Config.Name: Output Storage Path
Config.Description: Path where generated images and videos are stored. These are served back to GM Engine on your local machine.

Config.Name: SearXNG Secret Key
Config.Description: Internal secret key for SearXNG. The default value is fine — only change it if you want a custom key. Never exposed externally.

Config.Name: CivitAI API Key
Config.Description: Your CivitAI API key for downloading LoRA models. Generate one at civitai.red/user/account under API Keys. Required for the LoRA download step in GM Engine setup.

Config.Name: Enable Wan2.1 Video Generation
Config.Description: Set to true to enable Wan2.1 video generation. Note: on a P2000, video generation is slow (minutes per clip). ComfyUI image generation is always enabled regardless of this setting.

Config.Name: Ollama Model Directory
Config.Description: Path inside the container where Ollama stores models. Leave as default unless you have a specific reason to change it.

Config.Name: ComfyUI Model Directory
Config.Description: Path inside the container where ComfyUI looks for checkpoints, LoRAs, and other model files. Leave as default unless you have a specific reason to change it.
